chunks.text: Ilkin Isler, Resume - Page 1
ILKIN ISLERan
ilkinisler@gmail.com | www.linkedin.com/in/ilkinsevgiisler
Ph.D. in Computer Science with deep expertise in AI and deep learning. Experienced in designing and
deploying robust, explainable AI systems for high-risk applications. Skilled in computer vision, uncertainty
quantification, model interpretability, and advanced NLP workflows including retrieval-augmented
generation (RAG), citation systems, hallucination detection, and large language models (LLMs). Highly
adaptable and passionate about continuous learning, with a strong drive to apply AI and machine learning to
solve complex challenges and create meaningful impact.
• Ph.D., Computer Science, University of Central Florida, Orlando, FL Aug 2025
• MS, Computer Science, University of Central Florida, Orlando, FL Dec 2022
• BS, Computer Science, Hacettepe University, Ankara, Turkey June 2020
Senior AI Engineer Aug 2025 - Present
Universal Creative, AI Strategy & Solutions Orlando,
• Architected and built a secure, production-grade MCP-based AI platform from scratch, enabling
centralized orchestration and lifecycle management of AI agents and tools. Designed and implemented
role-based access control (RBAC), authentication layers, and security hardening mechanisms to ensure
safe deployment of ML services at scale. Delivered an end-to-end infrastructure stack supporting data
ingestion, model integration, API interfaces, and governed execution of AI workflows.
AI Software Engineer Jan - Aug 2025
Universal Creative, Engineering & Safety Orlando,
• Developed AI solutions using LLMs and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) with capabilities for
hallucination detection, groundedness evaluation, citation, meta tagging and topic modeling.
Graduate Teaching Assistant Aug 2023 - Dec 2024
University of Central Florida, College of Engineering and Computer Science Orlando,
• Assisting with Advanced Artificial Intelligence and Advanced Computer Networks courses.
Graduate Research Assistant Jan 2020 - Aug 2023
University of Central Florida, College of Engineering and Computer Science Orlando,
• Built explainable, uncertainty-aware models for organ and tumor segmentation and outcome prediction.
• Led cross-institutional projects (including Northwestern Radiology) to deploy AI visualization tools.
• Oversaw project delivery and documentation to support clinical integration of AI models.
Python Developer May - Oct
Universal Orlando Resort, BAIE Department Orlando,
• Enhanced system health and stability by identifying and resolving 100+ vulnerabilities in cloud scripts.
• Optimized machine learning pipelines with Azure Databricks and MLflow to enhance performance.
Data Analyst Jan - Aug
Simsoft Information Technologies Ankara, Turkey
• Implemented LSTM models for predictive maintenance and anomaly detection.
• Simulated COVID-19 spread using models to analyze disease transmission and population impact.
Research Intern Jul - Sep 2019
University of Central Florida, Synthetic Reality Lab Orlando,
• Utilized Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) for facial expression recognition.
Data Analyst Intern Jul - Sep 2018
Ilkin Isler, Resume - Page 2
Carbon Health San Francisco,
• Utilized machine learning to predict outcomes, validate data and reduce clinic wait times by 30%,
improving appointment forecasting and overall efficiency.
Languages: Python, Java, C, SQL
ML Systems & Infrastructure: MCP-based AI systems architecture, distributed ML pipelines, model
serving APIs, RBAC, secure AI deployment, model governance,
Areas of Expertise: Computer Vision; NLP (LLMs, RAG, hallucination detection); Uncertainty

chunks.source_title: The Mind to Move Mountains (UCF News)
chunks.text: The Mind to Move Mountains

chunks.source_title: The Mind to Move Mountains (UCF News)
chunks.text: Ilkin Isler '22MS '25PhD left the familiarity of home in Turkey to study AI at UCF - a daring choice that resulted in research improving the assessment of cancerous tumors and supporting better patient care.

chunks.source_title: The Mind to Move Mountains (UCF News)
chunks.text: Nothing motivates Ilkin Isler '22MS '25PhD quite like this phrase: “It can't be done.”

chunks.source_title: The Mind to Move Mountains (UCF News)
chunks.text: When Isler says candidly, “Working on my Ph.D. is the most difficult thing I've ever faced,” there's a montage of difficult things in her life to consider - physical and mental, past and present.

chunks.source_title: The Mind to Move Mountains (UCF News)
chunks.text: For example, in August 2025, the recent computer science doctoral graduate began work as a senior artificial intelligence (AI) engineer at Universal Creative. Her role involved analyzing complex white papers and developing state-of-the-art AI systems from scratch - all for the purpose of improving the safety of park guests.

chunks.source_title: The Mind to Move Mountains (UCF News)
chunks.text: “I find solutions for high-risk applications,” Isler says, “which is a good description for what I love to do.”

chunks.source_title: The Mind to Move Mountains (UCF News)
chunks.text: “… when I hear something can't be done, it motivates me to make it happen.” - Ilkin Isler '22MS '25PhD

chunks.source_title: The Mind to Move Mountains (UCF News)
chunks.text: The fact that her work requires fortitude also fits Isler well. In high school in Turkey, she once walked into a gym “just for something to do.” A trainer immediately suggested she head to the cardio area, where she'd blend in easily. Isler, however, saw the powerlifting space and thought, “ That's what I want to do.”

chunks.source_title: The Mind to Move Mountains (UCF News)
chunks.text: “I had to teach myself how to do [powerlifting],” she says. “People probably thought I'd give up, but when I hear something can't be done, it motivates me to make it happen.”

chunks.source_title: The Mind to Move Mountains (UCF News)
chunks.text: Self-taught and competing despite her small frame, Isler went on to become a European powerlifting champion.

chunks.source_title: The Mind to Move Mountains (UCF News)
chunks.text: All of this is important for understanding the person who, while studying at UCF, helped develop an AI model to assess cancerous tumors.

chunks.source_title: The Mind to Move Mountains (UCF News)
chunks.section: Using Computer Imaging to Enhance Patient Care
chunks.text: Guided by a team of advisors, Isler came up with a way to feed medical imaging into an algorithm that measures shapes and textures with more speed, precision and consistency than is currently possible. Given such accurate information, an oncologist could make quicker, more confident decisions - improving overall patient care.

chunks.source_title: The Mind to Move Mountains (UCF News)
chunks.section: Using Computer Imaging to Enhance Patient Care
chunks.text: “… [Ilkin's] work helps push the field forward in an important and timely way.” - David Mohaisen, UCF computer science professor

chunks.source_title: The Mind to Move Mountains (UCF News)
chunks.section: Using Computer Imaging to Enhance Patient Care
chunks.text: “The problem she chose to work on is inherently difficult for several reasons,” says David Mohaisen, UCF computer science professor and one of her advisors. “Medical imaging is a high-stakes domain where errors have serious clinical consequences.”

chunks.source_title: The Mind to Move Mountains (UCF News)
chunks.section: Using Computer Imaging to Enhance Patient Care
chunks.text: “It's also a mature and crowded research area, with competitive works focusing on incremental benchmark gains rather than addressing deeper issues, such as reliability, uncertainty and clinical integration,” Mohaisen continues. “Instead of chasing marginal improvements, Ilkin focused on meaningful outcomes. Her work helps push the field forward in an important and timely way.”

chunks.source_title: The Mind to Move Mountains (UCF News)
chunks.section: Using Computer Imaging to Enhance Patient Care
chunks.text: Yet the breakthrough alone, and the work that went into it, does not fully capture what Isler faced at the dizzying start of her doctorate journey.

chunks.source_title: The Mind to Move Mountains (UCF News)
chunks.section: Taking a Chance to Change Her Life and Others
chunks.text: Isler arrived from Turkey with two suitcases and a hotel reservation near UCF.

chunks.source_title: The Mind to Move Mountains (UCF News)
chunks.section: Taking a Chance to Change Her Life and Others
chunks.text: “My mom filled one of the suitcases with Turkish food,” Isler says. She can laugh now, but at the time, she had no clear path beyond the airport. “I had to figure everything out.”

chunks.source_title: The Mind to Move Mountains (UCF News)
chunks.section: Taking a Chance to Change Her Life and Others
chunks.text: A few years earlier, she traveled across the globe to UCF as an undergraduate, having earned a competitive internship in the Synthetic Reality Lab, where she worked with Pegasus Professor Charles Hughes and then computer engineering doctoral student Kamran Ali '21PhD on facial expression recognition.

chunks.source_title: The Mind to Move Mountains (UCF News)
chunks.section: Taking a Chance to Change Her Life and Others
chunks.text: “She committed herself to the research,” Hughes says, “and even contributed to writing and editing the resulting report after she returned to Turkey.”

chunks.source_title: The Mind to Move Mountains (UCF News)
chunks.section: Taking a Chance to Change Her Life and Others
chunks.text: Days away from starting doctorate-level research, with all her belongings in a single piece of luggage, Isler would do what she does best: find a way. She moved from the hotel to an unfurnished apartment.

chunks.source_title: The Mind to Move Mountains (UCF News)
chunks.section: Taking a Chance to Change Her Life and Others
chunks.text: But with her, Isler brought two specific interests to her postgraduate research at UCF. Her mother, a dentist in Turkey, inspired an interest in medical advancement. Her father, a computer science professor, recognized in his daughter the curiosity and determination required for the field.

chunks.source_title: The Mind to Move Mountains (UCF News)
chunks.section: Taking a Chance to Change Her Life and Others
chunks.text: “If I know an idea can be impactful, I'll do whatever is necessary to make it a reality,” she says. “The higher the stakes, the more driven I become.”

chunks.source_title: The Mind to Move Mountains (UCF News)
chunks.section: Taking a Chance to Change Her Life and Others
chunks.text: Those stakes led her to focus on medical imaging solutions for cancer care worldwide. Without a medical background, she collaborated with doctors. It helped that she never looked at her research as an academic exercise. She wanted only to deliver something trustworthy that clinicians everywhere could use to eliminate gray areas in image analysis.

chunks.source_title: The Mind to Move Mountains (UCF News)
chunks.section: Taking a Chance to Change Her Life and Others
chunks.text: “I've been fortunate to work with many doctoral students over the years, and Ilkin stands out on multiple levels,” says Mohaisen, pointing out her motivation, energy and curiosity, before adding: “I'd say she is among the most driven.”

chunks.source_title: The Mind to Move Mountains (UCF News)
chunks.text: By now, the source of Isler's uncommon drive is clear. Every time someone showed skepticism about her powerlifting, she responded by breaking a national record. Although she could have attended a private college in Turkey, where she would have been surrounded by familiar language and culture, she instead chose to move across the world to study AI.

chunks.source_title: The Mind to Move Mountains (UCF News)
chunks.text: There's one more motivation that consistently guides her: others.

chunks.source_title: The Mind to Move Mountains (UCF News)
chunks.text: Isler considers her greatest accomplishment in powerlifting to be the doors she helped open for others in Turkey to try any form of fitness they choose. And she says she would gladly move to another continent again, with just a suitcase and her AI skills, if it meant helping improve the lives of others.

chunks.source_title: The Mind to Move Mountains (UCF News)
chunks.text: “When I look back, I can see that I've often traded comfort for growth,” she says, “and I will not hesitate to do it again.”
I moved from Turkey to the United States to pursue AI at UCF, where I earned my MS in Computer Science in 2022 and my PhD in 2025.

I have been building trustworthy AI systems and LLM/RAG workflows for high-impact environments, including uncertainty-aware decision support, explainability, and hallucination-aware pipelines. I am also building personal AI assistants for home workflows and daily automation.

My research focuses on medical imaging, explainable AI, and uncertainty modeling, with the goal of building systems clinicians and real-world operators can trust. During my PhD, my dissertation centered on advanced AI algorithms for medical imaging, including tumor and organ-at-risk segmentation. More recently, I have been working on LLM and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) systems with hallucination detection, groundedness evaluation, citation, meta tagging, and topic modeling.
My powerlifting PRs are Squat 355 lbs, Bench 200 lbs, and Deadlift 475 lbs. I am a European powerlifting champion and a national champion in Turkey.
My featured story is The Mind to Move Mountains (UCF News): https://www.ucf.edu/news/the-mind-to-move-mountains/.
You can reach me at ilkinisler@gmail.com. LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/ilkinsevgiisler/. Google Scholar: https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=ZgPdlJ0AAAAJ&hl=en.
